valuation_summary: Quant DCF intrinsic value range is $68 (bear) / $94 (base) / $124 (bull) vs current price $201.68 — market is ~114% above base case and above every cell of the 5x5 sensitivity grid (max $142). No margin of safety exists on price; the only cushion is business quality.
compact_checklist: Business: NVIDIA sells GPU-plus-CUDA "AI factory" systems — 91% of FY2026's $215.9B revenue came from Data Center hardware bought by roughly a dozen hyperscalers and sovereign AI labs who keep buying because Blackwell/Rubin racks are the fastest way to turn electricity into trained model weights and inference tokens.

Moat: CUDA's ~4M developers and 40,000+ organizations running production code on NVIDIA's stack, combined with full-rack integration (NVLink + InfiniBand + Spectrum-X + reference designs) that makes the unit of competition a rack-scale system rather than a chip — proven in 71% gross margin and 87% ROIC that neither AMD nor hyperscaler ASICs have yet forced down.

Economics: FY2026 delivered 87.3% ROIC, 60.4% operating margin, $96.7B of free cash flow on $215.9B revenue (44.8% FCF margin), and ~$117B of owner earnings on only ~$2.8B of maintenance capex — this is as capital-light a cash engine as exists at mega-cap scale.

Management: Founder-CEO Jensen Huang (33 years in the seat) directly owns ~854M shares worth ~$172B, runs a 3.0%-of-revenue SBC program that buybacks of $40.1B in FY2026 more than offset, and has one proven mega-deal (Mellanox at $6.9B) plus one still-unproven one (Groq at ~$20B) — the meaningful blemish is that buybacks accelerated from $10B (FY23) to $40B (FY26) as the stock rose, i.e. buying more at higher multiples.

Debt risk: $62.6B cash against $11.0B debt (net cash $51.5B), 547x interest coverage, 3.9x current ratio, zero refinancing wall, and enough liquidity to run 2-3 years with zero revenue — a 30-50% revenue drawdown would hurt earnings but cannot threaten solvency.

Price: At $201.68 you must believe NVDA grows revenue ~28% CAGR for five years (to ~$745B by FY31) with no margin compression and an 18x+ terminal EV/EBITDA, because the quant DCF's base IV is $94, bull IV $124, the full 5x5 sensitivity grid tops out at $142.12, and 0 of 10,000 Monte Carlo runs produced an IV above today's price.

Margin of safety: There is none on price (-197% vs bear IV, -114% vs base IV, -63% vs bull IV) — the only cushion is the business itself: a fortress balance sheet, 87% ROIC, and a franchise that can absorb a 40-50% drawdown without permanent capital impairment, but you are carrying all the valuation risk.

Thesis breaker: Sell if two consecutive quarters of Data Center revenue growth below 20% YoY coincide with a top-2 hyperscaler publicly shifting a large training workload to custom silicon or ROCm, OR gross margin falls below 65% outside a product-cycle transition, OR Jensen Huang departs without a named successor.

No user-provided primary research in context folder.
